---
description: "Scaffold a new NixOS or nix-darwin host with all required files. Use when adding a new machine to the flake."
agent: "agent"
argument-hint: "Hostname, OS type (NixOS/Darwin), username, hardware details"
---

Create a new host following the [project conventions](../copilot-instructions.md).

## 1. Gather requirements

If not provided, ask for:

- **Hostname** — UPPERCASE (e.g., "RAVEN")
- **Username** — lowercase (e.g., "raven")
- **System** — `x86_64-linux`, `aarch64-linux`, `x86_64-darwin`, or `aarch64-darwin`
- **Channel** — `stable` or `unstable`
- **Time zone** — default: `Europe/Amsterdam`
- **UID** — numeric user ID (required for Darwin, recommended for NixOS)
- **Desktop environment** — NixOS only: `kde-plasma`, `cosmic`, or none
- **Starting modules** — optional list of extra modules to include beyond the base set

## 2. Create host directory

Create `hosts/<HOSTNAME>/` with all required files.

Use existing hosts as templates:

- NixOS: [hosts/FENNEC/](../../hosts/FENNEC/) for structure and format
- Darwin: [hosts/EDGE/](../../hosts/EDGE/) for structure and format

### user-settings.nix

```nix
{
  username = "<username>";
  hostname = "<HOSTNAME>";
  system = "<system>";
  channel = "<channel>";
  timeZone = "<timeZone>";
  # desktopEnvironment = "kde-plasma"; # NixOS only, omit for Darwin
  # uid = <uid>; # Required for Darwin, recommended for NixOS
}
```

Include `desktopEnvironment` only for NixOS hosts. Include `uid` always.

### configuration/default.nix

Start with a minimal module set, organized by category:

**NixOS base:**

- Host: `hardware-configuration.nix`, `configuration.nix`, `user.nix`
- Nix: `enable-flakes.nix`, `garbage.nix`
- System: `packages.nix`, `shared/bash.nix`, `system/console.nix`, `system/time.nix`, `system/i18n.nix`, `system/fonts.nix`

**Darwin base:**

- Host: `configuration.nix`, `user.nix`
- System: `shared/bash.nix`, `garbage.nix`, `packages.nix`, `fonts.nix`

Add any extra modules the user requested. Follow the category ordering from existing hosts (Nix, Bootloader, Hardware, Memory, System, Display, Peripherals, Services, Apps, Gaming).

### configuration/configuration.nix

Minimal host-specific config:

```nix
{ userSettings, ... }:
{
  networking.hostName = userSettings.hostname;
}
```

### configuration/user.nix

NixOS user setup with `initialPassword = userSettings.username`:

```nix
{ pkgs, userSettings, ... }:
{
  users.users.${userSettings.username} = {
    isNormalUser = true;
    initialPassword = userSettings.username;
    uid = userSettings.uid;
    home = "/home/${userSettings.username}";
    shell = pkgs.bashInteractive;
    extraGroups = [ "wheel" "networkmanager" ];
  };
  environment.shells = [ pkgs.bashInteractive ];
}
```

For Darwin, use the nix-darwin user pattern (no `isNormalUser`, no `initialPassword`, requires `users.knownUsers`).

### configuration/hardware-configuration.nix (NixOS only)

Create a placeholder:

```nix
# Auto-generated by nixos-generate-config — do not edit manually.
# Generate on the target machine:
#   nixos-generate-config --show-hardware-config > hosts/<HOSTNAME>/configuration/hardware-configuration.nix
{ ... }:
{
  # Placeholder — replace with actual hardware-configuration.nix
}
```

### home-manager/default.nix

Start with the base home-manager modules:

```nix
{ ... }:
{
  imports = [
    ./home.nix
    ../../../modules/home-manager/all/base.nix
    ../../../modules/home-manager/all/aliases.nix
    ../../../modules/home-manager/all/bash.nix
    ../../../modules/home-manager/all/git.nix
    ../../../modules/home-manager/all/ssh.nix
    ../../../modules/home-manager/all/starship.nix
  ];

  custom.hmBase.enable = true;
  custom.hmAliases.enable = true;
  custom.bash.enable = true;
  custom.git.enable = true;
  custom.ssh.enable = true;
  custom.starship.enable = true;
  custom.starship.style = "pastel-powerline";
}
```

Add Linux aliases module for NixOS hosts. Add any extra HM modules the user requested.

### home-manager/home.nix

```nix
{ userSettings, ... }:
{
  home.username = userSettings.username;
  home.homeDirectory = "/home/${userSettings.username}"; # Use /Users/ for Darwin
  home.stateVersion = "25.05"; # Use the current stable version
}
```

### README.md

Create a host README with hardware overview skeleton:

```markdown
# <HOSTNAME>

## Status

Configured — registered in `flake/hosts.nix`. Pending `hardware-configuration.nix` generation on actual hardware.

## Hardware

<!-- Fill in hardware details: motherboard, CPU, GPU, RAM, storage -->

## Useful Commands

\`\`\`bash

# Rebuild system

sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#<HOSTNAME>

# Update user config

home-manager switch --flake .#<HOSTNAME>
\`\`\`
```

## 3. Register in flake

Add the host to [flake/hosts.nix](../../flake/hosts.nix):

- NixOS → `nixosHosts` block
- Darwin → `darwinHosts` block

Include rebuild command comments in the same format as existing entries:

```nix
# `sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#<HOSTNAME>`
# `home-manager switch --flake .#<HOSTNAME>`
<HOSTNAME> = mkHost "<HOSTNAME>";
```

## 4. Update documentation

Update 3-4 files:

### README.md (root)

- Add row to hosts table (sorted alphabetically)
- Add rebuild + HM switch commands to Quick Commands section

### NIXOS.md or DARWIN.md

- Add row to hosts table
- Add rebuild command example

### HOME-MANAGER.md

- Add row to hosts table
- Add switch command example

## 5. Print next steps

After scaffolding, print:

> Host **<HOSTNAME>** scaffolded. Next steps:
>
> 1. Generate `hardware-configuration.nix` on the target machine:
>    `nixos-generate-config --show-hardware-config > hosts/<HOSTNAME>/configuration/hardware-configuration.nix`
> 2. Add hardware-specific modules (CPU, GPU, bootloader) using `/add-module`
> 3. Commit and push
